title: Estate Agents Say Rightmove Charges Too Much Money
summary: Estate agents are upset because Rightmove, a big website for listing houses, is charging them very high fees. Some agents say the fees have more than doubled in recent years, and they are struggling to pay. A group of estate agents, led by an accountant named Jeremy Newman, is taking Rightmove to court. They claim Rightmove is using its strong position in the market to charge too much. Rightmove says the claim is not true and they will fight it. They believe they offer good value to agents and people looking for homes. Many agents feel they have to use Rightmove because it brings them most of their customers, even though it is expensive. This situation is causing agents to hire fewer people and provide less effective services.

keywords.explanation: people who help buy and sell houses
a website where houses are listed for sale
money paid for using a service
a place where legal cases are decided
the area where buying and selling happens

options: It brings most of their customers

questions.question: What problem are estate agents facing because of high fees?

background_read: Estate agents are professionals who help people buy and sell properties. Rightmove is a popular online platform in the UK where agents list homes for sale. Fees are payments made regularly for services. A court case is a legal dispute where people argue their side before a judge. The market refers to the business environment where properties are traded. This article talks about a conflict over money between agents and Rightmove, affecting how agents run their businesses.
Article_Structure: WHO: Estate agents and Jeremy Newman
WHAT: Accusing Rightmove of charging too high fees and taking it to court
WHERE: In the UK property market
WHY: Because agents feel squeezed by rising costs and believe Rightmove is abusing its power

perspectives.description: They think Rightmove's fees are too high and unfair, hurting their ability to hire staff and serve customers effectively.
